title: carduus collinus subsp. cylindricus
intro: Carduus collinus subsp. cylindricus is a subspecies of the Carduus collinus plant, also known as the Hoary Cudweed, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Carduus collinus subsp. cylindricus include its ability to support digestive health, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or oil to create teas, tinctures, or salves for topical application.
